article_meta.title: Food and Drinks Published December 3, 2025 7:45am EST San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"'ADDICTIVE' ULTRA-PROCESSED FOODS LINKED TO SPIKE IN CHRONIC DISEASE, RESEARCHERS WARN","explanation":"This interstitial headline, chosen by the author/editors, uses alarming language ('SPIKE', 'WARN') and frames the plaintiff's claims as established fact, giving structural emphasis to the lawsuit's narrative over the defense perspective."},{"biasType":"Sensationalism and Emotionalism","biasedText":"SPIKE IN DEADLY CANCER BEFORE 50 LINKED TO COMMON CONVENIENCE FOODS","explanation":"This interstitial headline uses sensational language ('DEADLY CANCER', 'SPIKE') to shock readers, framing the issue in the most alarming terms possible."},{"biasType":"Sensationalism and Emotionalism","biasedText":"FOR THE FIRST TIME, THERE ARE MORE CHILDREN IN THE WORLD WHO ARE OBESE THAN UNDERWEIGHT","explanation":"This interstitial headline is inserted by the author/editors and uses a shocking comparative framing to heighten emotional impact, reinforcing the plaintiff's narrative without corresponding context."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"FOX Business reached out to all 10 companies named in the lawsuit but did not immediately hear back.","explanation":"While the article does include industry association responses, none of the 10 actual defendant companies are given a voice. The article devotes substantially more space and structural prominence to the plaintiff's claims, scientific studies supporting the lawsuit, and government findings than to the defense perspective, which is limited to two industry trade group statements placed in the latter portion of the article."}]}

detections.biasedText: 'ADDICTIVE' ULTRA-PROCESSED FOODS LINKED TO SPIKE IN CHRONIC DISEASE, RESEARCHERS WARN
detections.explanation: This interstitial headline, chosen by the author/editors, uses alarming language ('SPIKE', 'WARN') and frames the plaintiff's claims as established fact, giving structural emphasis to the lawsuit's narrative over the defense perspective.

detections.biasedText: SPIKE IN DEADLY CANCER BEFORE 50 LINKED TO COMMON CONVENIENCE FOODS
detections.explanation: This interstitial headline uses sensational language ('DEADLY CANCER', 'SPIKE') to shock readers, framing the issue in the most alarming terms possible.

detections.biasedText: FOR THE FIRST TIME, THERE ARE MORE CHILDREN IN THE WORLD WHO ARE OBESE THAN UNDERWEIGHT
detections.explanation: This interstitial headline is inserted by the author/editors and uses a shocking comparative framing to heighten emotional impact, reinforcing the plaintiff's narrative without corresponding context.

detections.biasedText: FOX Business reached out to all 10 companies named in the lawsuit but did not immediately hear back.
detections.explanation: While the article does include industry association responses, none of the 10 actual defendant companies are given a voice. The article devotes substantially more space and structural prominence to the plaintiff's claims, scientific studies supporting the lawsuit, and government findings than to the defense perspective, which is limited to two industry trade group statements placed in the latter portion of the article.
